status_detail: APF blocking appears to WORK for HuggingFace. Using autofocus-paste strategy (no click - pure Ctrl+V on page load), the prompt was successfully submitted: URL changed from https://huggingface.co/chat to https://huggingface.co/chat/conversation/69e6d69401bd3baf1bc66b20 (a new conversation ID was generated), tab title changed from 'HuggingChat' to 'New Chat'. However, the main chat panel is EMPTY in af_04_final.png — neither the user's prompt message nor any AI response/error is rendered. This matches the BLOCKED_SILENT_RESET pattern documented in browser-rules.md: prompt POST reached the service, service created the conversation resource, but either the request body was blocked at APF layer (h2_end_stream=2) or the streaming response was terminated, and HuggingChat frontend silently cleared the UI rather than showing an error. No Korean warning text rendered to the user — this is a 'block succeeded, warning not displayed' case, which is the exact scenario the pipeline Phase 6 (apf-warning-impl) is designed to address via C++ generator function.

network_evidence.chat_area_state: empty (no user message, no AI response, no error)

notes_for_dev: h2_end_stream=2 retry appears effective at blocking the prompt, but the user sees an empty chat panel instead of a warning message. This is the expected handoff point to apf-warning-impl: C++ generator function needs to inject an HTTP-level response that HuggingChat frontend will render as visible warning content. Recommend next action: design warning injection for huggingface.co/chat/conversation/<id> endpoint (server-sent events response stream). The conversation ID format suggests HF uses UUIDs - the warning can be delivered as an SSE event with role=assistant containing the Korean warning text.
retry_context: Retry of #498 after reload_services re-executed and h2_end_stream=2 confirmed. Retry successfully validates the block effect; warning rendering is the remaining work.
automation_method: autofocus_paste (no mouse click; relies on HuggingChat autofocusing its textarea on page load)
